test_suite.details: node test.js: 3519/3519 passed. Schema, internal-link, build-pipeline, prose, prediction-panel checks all green. Test 8 covers 79 prediction panels (12 tombstone, 67 mined, 10 operational).

Spot-check confirmed all 12 unique showTab args (overview, evaluate, model, selftest, wins, pages, killshots, timestamp, predictions, falsify, ai, refs) resolve to one of the 12 ds-tab-content divs; 0 args without matching tab.

Carry-forward podaac-tools timeout is host-specific. No content-level broken data sources.

theme_color_drift.details: 0 hardcoded light backgrounds (#fff, #f8f9fa, etc.) in sections.json inline styles. 3 occurrences of color:#555 in inline italic-subheader styles (sections.json part4.html, part5.html, part6.html) — minor, could be replaced with var(--text) but does not break dark theme. Carry-forward from prior runs.

discoverability_infrastructure.details: docs/llms.txt (5,628 B) opens with our review URL, mentions the dome model by name, references current 71/69 counts. docs/sitemap.xml is valid XML with main URL + llms.txt URL. docs/robots.txt has Allow:/ and Sitemap: directive pointing to our sitemap. <head> meta: description, og:title, og:description, ld+json ClaimReview block all present. data/uncounted-failures.json schema: 10 entries (FAIL-001..FAIL-010), all with id/dome_ref/dome_label/what_actually_happened, no duplicates. dome_claimed_failures=4 matches scorecard.

expansion_tracker_continuity.details: FUSE and git canonical now agree (md5 match: 2f14704bde1b8191358421f8cfe7335f). next_id=287, max_id=286, 285 items — collision-safe. Prior run's FUSE staleness (next_id=285 vs git 286) has been resolved — the 2026-05-04 13:06Z workspace-sync run propagated the canonical state back. Single ID gap at EXP-052 is long-standing carry-forward. 0 strict orphans; 0 phantoms; 0 stale-pending. 171 non-EXP files (ISS-*, MODE5-*, PRED-assessment-*, etc.) in expansions/ are legitimate artifacts from other pipelines. 0 coupled-category items need queue presence.

workspace_only_files.details: FUSE has 461 review files vs git's 460 — only diff is WIN-067.c3.json.build.js (empty stray, name-mangled debris from a node script — not data). FUSE expansions/ has 1 extra file: EXP-202.json.bak (canonical EXP-202.json present in git). Both debris files are carry-forward; not data-loss-critical, but recommend cleanup. No legitimate workspace-only data files at risk.

workspace_sync_status.details: workspace-sync has not run since 2026-05-04T13:06:22Z (last entry in monitor/integrity/workspace-sync-runs/). Current time 2026-05-05T08:12Z = ~19h since last run. Schedule is every 4h, so ~4 runs missed. Not as bad as the prior incident (27h) but the agent is once again drifting from its schedule. The most recent run cadence (every 1h between 05:41 and 13:06 on 2026-05-04) suggests it was running for a window then stopped. Out of strict scope for structure-integrity but reported because the agent's state directly affects FUSE/git divergence and the prompt's section 7d covers the skip log this agent produces.

workspace_sync_skip_log.details: Last skip-log entry 2026-05-04T13:05:31Z. 41 entries in last-24h window — all mtime-guard skips (informational, normal). 0 direction-violation skips. Mtime-guard repetitions are expected for files like review-state.json that decider commits directly to git. No boundary-crossing bugs detectable from the log.

Unprocessed: 3 (WIN-016.c6.json, WIN-017.c3.json, WIN-021.c3.json) — all created in the last ~7h (2026-05-05 01:23, 06:48, 02:41 UTC), within normal pipeline lag. 0 parse errors in errors[]. Digest also surfaces 233 'under-covered processed' reviews flagging 1256 missing-issues holes — informational, surfaced for decider/tinker, not in this prompt's scope.

broken_review_files.details: 0 review files in errors[] from regenerated digest. Auto-recovery command not needed.

The single bare 69 count ('the model claims 69 confirmed predictions') is the dome's own claim, accompanied by the live query for our review's count, so it complies with the no-hardcoded-counts rule.

Output md5 8c0e6e152cec00179e78e83f2af4140f matches committed docs/index.html in FUSE and git byte-for-byte. Generated HTML size 1453.2 KB. No source-vs-published drift. Clone deleted at end of run.

issues_found.description: data/sections.json part1b 'new-content-breakdown' TLDR says 'The 30 new WINs break into recognizable patterns' — actual new_in_v51 = 32. The detail body two paragraphs later correctly templates {{NEW_IN_V51}} = 32, so the same <details> block is internally inconsistent. The sub-bucket sums (9+5+5+9 = 28) also do not reach 32 — needs both the TLDR count AND the breakdown numbers refreshed against the current set: WIN-040..069 + WIN-057b + WIN-058b = 32. Suggested re-bucket: geomagnetic 9 (WIN-040..043, 053, 059..061, 063), tidal 5 (WIN-045, 046, 049, 050, 051), cosmological 5 (WIN-047, 048, 052, 054, 055), miscellaneous 13 (WIN-044, 056, 057, 057b, 058, 058b, 062, 064..069) = 32.
issues_found.location: data/sections.json part1b html (rendered at docs/index.html line 1027)
issues_found.suggested_fix: Replace 'The 30 new WINs' → 'The {{NEW_IN_V51}} new WINs' in the TLDR and either drop the explicit per-bucket counts or update them to 9/5/5/13 = 32. Same finding flagged on 2026-05-04T05-39, 2026-05-04T08-20, and now 2026-05-05T08-12 — 4th carry-forward, no movement.

issues_found.description: dome-workspace-sync agent has not run since 2026-05-04T13:06:22Z (~19h ago). Schedule is every 4 hours, so ~4 runs missed. Cadence visible in monitor/integrity/workspace-sync-runs/ shows it was firing every hour from 05:41Z through 13:06Z on 2026-05-04 then stopped. Current FUSE/git state is in sync (md5 match on expansion-tracker.json), but the dormancy is a recurring pattern (also flagged 2026-05-04T08-20 with 27h dormancy) and is the same root cause that produced the prior FUSE-staleness bug.
issues_found.suggested_fix: Tinker should investigate why dome-workspace-sync stops firing after intermittent runs. Until restored, multi-writer files (expansion-tracker.json, review-state.json, attention-inbox.json) risk drifting. Recurrence rate suggests a scheduler bug, not a transient miss.

issues_found.description: podaac-tools.jpl.nasa.gov GRACE ProdSpec PDF — TCP timeout twice from this network (≥8 s). On 2026-05-04 the base host (podaac.jpl.nasa.gov) returned 200; today both that base host and the tools subhost timed out. Could be either a transient JPL-side network issue or a host-specific change worth flagging for next run.

issues_found.suggested_fix: rm both files. The .build.js name-mangled stray suggests a curmudgeon-side path-construction bug worth checking in monitor/prompts/curmudgeon.md.

summary: Site is healthy and the build is reproducible byte-for-byte (md5 8c0e6e15…). Test suite passes 3519/3519. FUSE and git are now back in sync (last run's FUSE staleness on expansion-tracker.json has been resolved — md5 match, next_id=287). The two recurring stories: (1) the 'The 30 new WINs' TLDR vs 32-actual mismatch is now on its 4th carry-forward and the breakdown buckets also don't sum to 32 — would benefit from a single decider patch; (2) dome-workspace-sync has stopped firing again after a brief healthy window (~19h dormant since 2026-05-04T13:06Z), a recurrence of the prior pattern. No critical issues. WIN-016.c6 / WIN-017.c3 / WIN-021.c3 are unprocessed but recent (within normal pipeline lag). Internal anchors / tab chain / progressive disclosure / discoverability infrastructure all green. No broken review files, no direction-violation skips.
